description: §41.14.5 — supplying BOTH pick=[...] AND omit=[...] on the same <formFor> fires E-FORMFOR-PICK-OMIT-CONFLICT. Both name VALID fields (pick=["name"], omit=["email"]), so this pins the mutual-exclusivity rule specifically, NOT a field-validity error — the two transforms are inverses (pick names the only fields to render; omit names fields to exclude) and cannot be combined. Because the fields are valid, E-FORMFOR-PICK-INVALID-FIELD / E-FORMFOR-OMIT-INVALID-FIELD MUST NOT fire.
rationale: §41.14.5 E-FORMFOR-PICK-OMIT-CONFLICT: pick= and omit= SHALL NOT be combined on the same <formFor> call. Fix: use exactly one per call, or layer Pick over Omit at the type level and pass the derived type.
